mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE reading above 6% indicates persistent labor market slack, which encourages the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative monetary policy. Lower discount rates and ample liquidity support equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% in Aug 2013 above 6% threshold
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains above 6% but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and rising HOUST signals that monetary accommodation is effectively stimulating housing demand, a key engine of economic recovery. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because it indicates robust aggregate demand without immediate tightening risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.5% and HOUST 3-month moving average increasing
regime_hint: monetary easing with nascent housing recovery
failure_condition: If both conditions are met but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences, or if the joint signal's predictive power is not significantly better than each series individually (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output and overall economic activity. In any regime, rising production is associated with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO annualized growth is positive (>0% month-over-month) but forward 60-day returns are negative in >40% of months over a 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL (consumer prices) above a 0.2% month-over-month threshold signals nascent inflationary pressure, which raises the probability of eventual Fed tightening. This tightening expectation compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL m/m > 0.2% but forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the current monetary easing cycle with FEDFUNDS near zero and UNRATE above 6%, increases in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) are interpreted as confirmation of a strengthening labor market without imminent tightening, since the Fed has committed to low rates until unemployment falls substantially. This positive labor demand shock boosts consumer spending expectations and equity returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it is only active when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and UNRATE > 6%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in Aug 2013, UNRATE at 7.3%
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and UNRATE > 6%
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by more than 150k in a month and forward 20-day returns are negative in >50% of such months while the regime conditions hold, reject.

mechanism_narrative: RSAFS (retail and food services sales) is a real-time proxy for consumer spending. Positive growth in RSAFS signals strong consumption demand, which supports corporate revenues and stock prices over short horizons. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but forward 5-day returns are negative in >55% of occurrences over a 1-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above the 900,000 unit level indicate a solid housing market recovery, which has strong multiplier effects on employment, construction spending, and consumer confidence. This threshold signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 896 in Jul 2013 (just below threshold)
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 900 but forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of months over a 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2%) and high unemployment (UNRATE > 6%) defines a 'slack' macro environment where the Fed is likely to remain accommodative. This joint condition signals positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in continued policy support.
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but forward 5-day returns are negative in >50% of days over a 2-year sample, reject.
